changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Satoko's Trap Course (Intermediate)"

TextENGNew: I could see Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun heading toward the gym storehouse.
They needed to fetch the bag of lime used to draw lines on the court for our next gym class.
They were on duty today, after all.
But... in that storehouse... a terrible trap was awaiting them...!!
"...Being on this side of a trap for once... is genuinely thrilling!"
"Ohohoho! If you appreciate that thrill, Keiichi-san, then you have real potential!"
The two of them fumbled around with the gym storehouse's lock.
......Oh?@k What's this?
Okamura-kun pointed at part of the door and started whispering to Tomita-kun.
Then, realizing something, they both jumped away from the door.
``Oh... Satoko, I think they found out! Did we mess up?!``
``...I had calculated that they would notice the trap at the entrance.
And, of course, I've calculated what action they will take now. Ho-ho-ho...``
According to Satoko... after the two of them noticed the suspicious device on the door, they would go around to the back and try to climb in through the window.
And just as she'd prophesized... they went around to the back...@k and just as she'd prophesized, they climbed up... opened the window and tried to sneak in...
``...Wow... amazing... Everything's exactly like you said...!!@k@vS20/01/440100106. ......Ah! It got them!!!``
Boof, poooooooooooooooof!!
A blast of pure white lime powder erupted from the window like an explosion.
...After a few moments, the two of them managed to crawl away from the window... both of them completely white.
``Ho-ho-ho! Ohh-ho-ho-ho!!!@k@vS20/04/440400058. This is the moment I live for~!@k@vS20/04/440400059. It tastes like honey~!!``
``...But still... ...That was some trap...

"......To set such an elaborate trap, you'd have to know exactly where Tomita-kun puts his hands when he climbs the window, where he puts his feet, where he jumps down—you'd have to anticipate all of it...?!"
changeReason: New translation adds the specific reference to Tomita-kun that was missing in the old, and more precisely captures the need to predict hand/foot placement for the trap.

TextENGNew: "Listen well, Keiichi-san. The first step in setting a trap is observing your target, you know?"
You must scrupulously predict their movement patterns—how they'll move at any given moment! If you do that, then you can achieve the greatest possible results with only the most minimal trap!``
...Come to think of it, Mion had once praised Satoko effusively, saying she had a natural genius for reading the final move in a critical moment.
``So it's all about reading the opponent. ...So then, the traps you've caught me in before—they worked because you figured out everything I'd do?``
``Yes. I can see right through all your actions, and that's how I set them up!
So if one day you suddenly started having different habits and behavior, I wouldn't be able to anymore.``
...Being told she could read me like an open book...@k...was a bit annoying, honestly.
``Okay, then. Let's play a game now.@k@vS20/01/440100111. I want to test whether your predictions are accurate.``
``Yes, that is fine with me. Keiichi-san, you are particularly easy to get a read on—I could do it before breakfast!@k@vS20/04/440400065. Ohh-ho-ho-ho!!``
When we went back to the classroom, Satoko tore off pieces of paper and wrote something on the back of them, making three cards.
Of course, I didn't know what she'd written.
``Please, pick one of these cards, Keiichi-san. If you do not pick one that says 'you're a loser,' you will win.``
``Great, you're on!! The club has built up my sensitivity to this stuff, so I'll smash right through your predictions!``
Satoko smirks and holds out the three cards.
Set your mind to full power by calming it, Keiichi Maebara!@k ...Cool and collected is how you'll win!
......If I don't pick the losing one, I'll win.
...Which means if I do pick it, Satoko will win.
......In other words, Satoko wants to make me pick the losing one.
What would she do... to make sure I picked the losing one?
Could she even predict something like that?!
Statistically speaking, isn't there some rule... that says I'm more likely to pick the one in the middle, or the ones on either side...?!
And then... it hits me like a flash—this is what Mion would do!
``Now, which one will you pick?``
``I've made my decision. ...And it is...... this ooonnneeeeeeeeeee!!``
``Huh? Ahhh!!@k@vS20/04/440400069. W-What are you doing?!``
I snatched all three cards from Satoko's hands at once!
I had turned Satoko's own logic against her.
changeReason: Original says 'predicted what Satoko would do' while the Japanese (逆に読んでやった) means 'read her in reverse/turned her logic against her' — the new captures the reversal aspect.

TextENGNew: ...Satoko wanted to make sure I picked the losing one.
Which meant... all three of these were losing ones!!
``And that's how I knew!! Allow me to look at all three!!!``
I flipped all three cards over... and on each one, a single character was written.
changeReason: Original says 'a single word' on each card while the Japanese (カタカナが１文字ずつ) specifies 'one katakana character each' — the new translation is more accurate about the puzzle mechanic.

TextENGNew: ``You're``@k ``A``@k ``Loser``
``It means just what it says. ...If you had only chosen one, you wouldn't have lost.``
``What... whaaaaaat?! T-Then... you predicted... that I'd draw all threeeeeeee?!
Gyaaaaaahhhh!!!``
I clutched my head in anguish while Satoko reveled at the peak of her triumph!
changeReason: Original says 'fainted' while the Japanese (悶絶) means 'writhing in agony/anguish' — a different physical reaction entirely.

TextENGNew: Her shrill laughter echoed throughout the classroom.
And then, there was a poof as lime powder came down upon Satoko's head.
I looked to see Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun, all in white, dumping a whole bag of lime powder on her, getting their revenge.
``Wha-?!@k@vS20/04/440400072. W-What are you suddenly trying to do, you two?!``
``...Looks like there's no use talking to the person who set up that trick.``
``Well, excuuuuse me!! Would you happen to have any proof?
And I'd like it if you didn't call it something so inelegant—a trick!
I would prefer you call it something more refined: a trap♪ please!``

That said, her bravado didn't last long -- the sight of two figures, completely white and speechless, approaching her in silence...@kWell, they couldn't speak even if they wanted to, with their mouths packed full of lime -- but the way they bore down on her was... admittedly terrifying, even for Satoko.
changeReason: The original translation is somewhat incoherent; the new translation clearly conveys the Japanese meaning — two lime-covered figures approaching in silence, unable to speak because their mouths are full of lime.

TextENGNew: ``Eeeeeeeeeeeeeek!!! K-K-Keiichi-san, h-help meee!!``
...Our underclassmen crashed around the classroom in furious combat, getting white lime everywhere.
...Hey, Satoko.@k...How come you can't predict outcomes like this?
What I hadn't foreseen at the time was that the teacher would come in afterward and give all of us a thorough scolding...
